Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 094 [Šumma Izbu 22][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 ⸢U₃⸣.[TU ...] ⸢E₂ BI⸣ BIRaḫ ta-⸢lit⸣-ti ⸢bu⸣-li₃ NU SI.SA₂

(o 1) [If a] sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) [...] that household will be scattered; the offspring of the livestock will not thrive.

o 22

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma x [...] x maš-ra-a i-⸢šar⸣-ru ne-me₂-lam IGImar ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.LA

(o 2) [If a sow] gives birth to 1 (piglet) and ... [...] ... he will get rich; he will experience profit; joy.

o 33

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU-⸢su⸣ [(x)] SU GE₆at LU₂ BI UKU₂

(o 3) [If a sow] gives birth to 1 (piglet) and its head [...] ... is black: that man will become poor.

o 44

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma BABBAR EN munusŠAḪ KISLAḪ₃ IGImar

(o 4) [If a sow] gives birth to 1 (piglet) and it is white: the sow's owner will experience distress.

o 55

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma GE₆ EN munusŠAḪ ne-me-lam IGImar

(o 5) [If] a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and it is black: the sow's owner will experience profit.

o 66

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma SA₅ ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.⸢LA?⸣

(o 6) [If] a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and it is red: joy.

o 77

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma SIG₇ DUMU! ⸢LUGAL⸣ AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DABbat

(o 7) [If] a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and it is yellow: the king's son will seize his father's throne.

o 88

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ ḫu-up-pu-da E₂ BI ŠUBdi EN munusŠAḪ UŠ₂ : ZAḪ₂

(o 8) [If] a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and its eyes are blind: that house will be abandoned; the sow's owner will die (or): disappear.

o 99

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ ⸢ZA₃?⸣ pu-us-su-la E₂ BI SI.SA₂ ina E₂ LU₂ MAŠKIM ZI.GA

(o 9) [If] a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and its front leg are crippled: that household will thrive; in the man's house, a demon will arise.

o 1010

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ EGIR-MEŠ ⸢ZA₃?⸣ [pu]-us-su-la E₂ BI TURer mim-mu-šu₂ i-lap-pi-in : i-rab-bi

(o 10) If a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and its rear legs are crippled on the right: that household will shrink; all of it will become poor (or): will grow.

o 1111

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma pu-us-su-la mim-mu LU₂ i-kam-mis

(o 11) If a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and (they) are crippled:1 the man will gather all (that belongs to him).

o 1212

[BE munus]⸢ŠAḪ⸣ 1 U₃.TU-ma i-ta-kal ⸢KUR⸣ su-un-qu dan-nu DABbat

(o 12) If a sow gives birth to 1 (piglet) and has eaten (it): a severe famine will afflict the land.

o 1313

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU ta-lit-ti munusŠAḪ la₃ GAL₂ši x x KISLAḪ₃ KUR DABbat MU-šu lem-niš IM x NU GAL₂ši

(o 13) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) (and) there is no sow offspring: ... distress will afflict the land; his name, with evil intent ... there will not be ....

o 1414

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU-ma nam-ru muš-ta-an-nu im-taḫ-ḫa-ru

(o 14) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) and they are bright: hostile (people) will attack each other.

o 1515

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU-ma 1 BABBAR 1 GE₆ muš-ta-an-nu 1 i-⸢bel?⸣-li <1> UKU₂ šum₄-ma ina ŠEŠ-MEŠ 1 NIG₂.TUK 1 ⸢UKU₂?⸣

(o 15) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) and 1 is white, 1 is black: (among) hostile people 1 will rule, 1 will become poor; if (it is among) brothers, 1 will be(come) rich, 1 will become poor.

o 1616

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU-ma 1 BABBAR 1 GE₆ u₃ BABBAR MI PA₃ RU PA x x LI lu KAL lu-u₂ i-šap-pil₂ ME x [...]

(o 16) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) and 1 is white, 1 is black and ....: either he will become strong or he will become depressed ... [...].

o 1717

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU-ma TEŠ₂.BI BABBAR-MEŠ at-ḫu-u₂ E₂ AD-šu₂-nu ana KU₃.BABBAR BUR₂-[MEŠ]

(o 17) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) and they are completely white: the brothers will dispose of their paternal house for silver.

o 1818

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU-ma TEŠ₂.BI GE₆-MEŠ E₂ BI ne-me-lam : NIG₂.TUK [...]

(o 18) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) and they are completely black: that house (will make)2 profit (or): it will [...] riches.

o 1919

BE munusŠAḪ 2 U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu-nu E₂ BI [...]

(o 19) If a sow gives birth to 2 (piglets) and their heads are 3: that house [...].

o 2020

BE munusŠAḪ 3 U₃.TU ana ra-ma-ni-šu₂ NU SIG₅ UMUŠ KUR MANni DAM u DAM KAD₃-MEŠ E₂ EN x [...]

(o 20) If a sow gives birth to 3 (piglets): for itself it will not become good; the decisions for the land will change; husband and wife will be tied; the owner's house ... [...].

o 2121

BE munusŠAḪ 3 U₃.TU-ma 2 BABBAR-MEŠ 1 GE₆ ina DUMU-MEŠ EN-šu₂ 1 ⸢SIG₅⸣ 2 [...]

(o 21) If a sow gives birth to 3 (piglets) and 2 are white, 1 is black: among its owner's sons, 1 will become good, 2 will [...].

o 2222

BE munusŠAḪ 3 U₃.TU-ma 2 GE₆-MEŠ 1 BABBAR ina DUMU-MEŠ EN-šu₂ 2 ⸢SIG₅⸣ 1 [...]

(o 22) If a sow gives birth to 3 (piglets) and 2 are black, 1 is white: among its owner's sons, 2 will become good, 1 will [...].

o 2323

BE munusŠAḪ 3 U₃.TU-ma SAG.KI-MEŠ BABBAR-MEŠ KUN-MEŠ GE₆-MEŠ u₂-nu-[ut?] [...]

(o 23) If a sow gives birth to 3 (piglets) and (their) foreheads are white, (their) tails are black: the equipment of [...].

o 2424

BE munusŠAḪ 3 U₃.TU-ma SAG.KI-MEŠ GE₆-MEŠ KUN-MEŠ BABBAR-MEŠ ⸢ŠA⸣ x x [...]

(o 24) If a sow gives birth to 3 (piglets) and (their) foreheads are black, (their) tails are white: ... [...].

o 2525

BE munusŠAḪ 4 U₃.TU at-ḫu-u₂ i-zu-uz-[zu] [x (x)] DAM [...]

(o 25) If a sow gives birth to 4 (piglets): the brothers will stand (by each other); [...] the wife [...].

o 2626

BE munusŠAḪ 4 U₃.TU-ma BABBAR <GE₆> SIG₇ u SA₅ TUKU UMUŠ KUR [BIR]aḫ E₂ [...]

(o 26) If a sow gives birth to 4 (piglets) and she has 1 white, 1 black, 1 yellow and 1 red: the decisions for the land will be scattered; the house [...].

o 2727

BE munusŠAḪ 5 U₃.TU [x (x)] AŠ ina KUR ŠUBut [...]

(o 27) If a sow gives birth to 5 (piglets): [...] ... will fall in the country.

o 2828

[BE] munusŠAḪ 5 U₃.TU-ma SAG.KI-su-nu [...] x GE₆ DUMU-MEŠ EGIR AD-šu-nu E₂ AD-šu₂-nu ana ⸢KU₃.BABBAR⸣ [BUR₂]

(o 28) If a sow gives birth to 5 (piglets) and their forehead [...] ... is black: after their father('s death), the sons will dipose of their paternal house for silver.

o 2929

[BE munusŠAḪ x U₃].⸢TU-ma⸣ SAG.DU-[su]-nu BABBAR DUMU-MEŠ EGIR AD-šu-nu E₂ AD-šu₂-nu i-ba-ʾi-i-lu

(o 29) [If a sow gives] birth to [x (piglets)] and their head is white: the sons, after their father('s death) will become dominant (in) their paternal house.

o 3030

[BE munusŠAḪ x U₃.TU]-⸢ma⸣ SAG [...] ša₂ EN mi₃-li GABA SIG₅ E₂ NA qe₂-ru-ub

(o 30) [If a sow gives birth to x (piglets)] and ... of the owner: a fine success will come near the man's house.

o 3131

⸢BE⸣ [...] x MAŠ-šu₂-nu GE₆ GI.ḪUL ana LU₂ GAL₂!(ZI-)šu₂ U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ ina ku-tal-lu₄ LU₂ DABbat!(.AŠ)

(o 31) If [...] ... they are half black: there will be a mourning ceremony for the man; his days will become short; he will be seized from behind.

o 3232

BE munusŠAḪ 6 U₃.TU-⸢ma⸣ SAG.KI-su-nu BABBAR-<<ME>> KUN-su-nu GE₆ DUMU-MEŠ AD-šu₂-nu i-ba-ʾi-lu SIG₅ E₂ NA šuk-lul

(o 32) If a sow gives birth to 6 (piglets) and their forehead is white, their tail is black: the sons will dominate their father; good fortune will be perfect in the man's house.

o 3333

BE munusŠAḪ 7 U₃.TU DUMU-MEŠ UGU AD-šu₂-nu i-šar-ru-u₂ mim-ma AŠ₂!(MA-)šu₂-nu KUR TUR₃ BI DAGALiš

(o 33) If a sow gives birth to 7 (piglets): the sons will become richer than their father; all of their wishes will be achieved; that animal stall will expand.

o 3434

BE munusŠAḪ 7 U₃.TU-ma TEŠ₂.BI BABBAR-MEŠ LU₂ i-za-ak-ku-u₂ GABA.UŠ

(o 34) If a sow gives birth to 7 (piglets) and they are completely white: an ambiguous omen (for) the man who is pure.

o 3535

BE munusŠAḪ 7 U₃.TU-ma SAG.KI-su-nu GE₆-<<ME>> KUN-su-nu BABBAR UKU₂ i-šar-ru

(o 35) If a sow gives birth to 7 (piglets) and their foreheads are black, their tails are white: the poor man will become rich.

o 3636

BE munusŠAḪ 8 U₃.TU ṭuḫ-du ina KUR GAL₂ at-ḫu-u NU ŠE.ŠE.GA

(o 36) If a sow gives birth to 8 (piglets): there will be abundance in the land; the brothers will not agree with one another.

o 3737

BE munusŠAḪ 9 U₃.TU E₂ NA NU SI.SA₂ UZU-BI ul im-aq!(DA-)qar

(o 37) If a sow gives birth to 9 (piglets): the man's house will not thrive; his meat will not become rare.

o 3838

BE munusŠAḪ 10 U₃.TU BURU₁₄ KUR SI.SA₂ EN-ša i-šar-ru

(o 38) If a sow gives birth to 10 (piglets): the land's harvest will thrive; its owner will become rich.

o 3939

BE munusŠAḪ 15 U₃.TU A₂.DAM LUGAL ana ŠA₃ IRI i-pa-ḫu-ru

(o 39) If a sow gives birth to 15 (piglets): the king's flocks will be gathered in the middle of the city.

o 4040

BE munusŠAḪ 20 U₃.TU nu-kur₂-tu₄ GID₂.DA

(o 40) If a sow gives birth to 20 (piglets): enmity will last long.

o 4141

BE munusŠAḪ 25 U₃.TU PAP SAG.KI KA-BI KIŠIB-BI DIḪ DABbat

(o 41) If a sow gives birth to 25 (piglets): a disease will afflict all of (his) forehead, his mouth (and) his hand.

o 4242

BE munusŠAḪ 30 U₃.TU nu-kur₂-tu₄ GAL₂-ma KA KUR LUGAL BAL

(o 42) If a sow gives birth to 30 (piglets): there will be enmity and the opinion of the land will turn against the king.

o 4343

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma BABBAR-MEŠ EN-ša₂ i-šar₂-ru₃ a-wi-lu-tu₂ DU⸢ak⸣

(o 43) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are white: its owner will become rich; he will reach an old age.

o 4444

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma GE₆-MEŠ EN-ša₂ BA.UŠ₂ : KISLAḪ₃ ⸢ZU?ad₂?⸣

(o 44) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are black: its owner will die; he will learn distress.

o 4545

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SA₅-MEŠ ḫu-ud lib₃-bi

(o 45) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are red: happiness.

o 4646

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma GUN₃-ME TUR₃ LU₂ BIRaḫ

(o 46) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are multicoloured: the man's animal stall will be scattered.

o 4747

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₇-ME EN-ša₂ ul-tab-bar

(o 47) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are yellow: its owner will live long.

o 4848

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ nu-uk-ku-⸢sa!⸣ E₂ BI BIRaḫ KUR ŠA₃.ḪUL IGI

(o 48) If a sow gives birth and their (the piglets') feet are torn: that household will be scattered; the land will experience sorrow.

o 4949

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ la₃ GAL₂-ME TUR₃ E₂ LU₂ BIRaḫ KUR SU.GU₇ IGImar

(o 49) If a sow gives birth and its feet do not exist: the animal stall of the man's house will be scattered; the land will experience hunger.

o 5050

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma 2 SAG.DU-ME-šu₂ EN munusŠAḪ UŠ₂ E₂ EN-šu₂ BIRaḫ

(o 50) If a sow gives birth and its (the piglet's) heads are 2: the sow's owner will die; its3 owner's household will be scattered.

o 5151

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-ME-šu₂ E₂ EN-ša₂ BIRaḫ

(o 51) If a sow gives birth and its (the piglet's) heads are 3: its owner's household will be scattered.

o 5252

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU la₃ GAL₂ LU₂ BI UŠ₂! x E₂ EN!-šu₂ BIRaḫ

(o 52) If a sow gives birth and there is no head: that man will die; its owner's household will be scattered.

o 5353

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂-nu nu-uk-ku-⸢sa⸣ EN-šu₂ UŠ₂-ma E₂ EN-šu₂ x SAG? GA UŠ-su E₂.GAL UŠde

(o 53) If a sow gives birth and their heads are torn: its owner will die and its owner's house will ...; the palace will appropriate his inheritance.

o 5454

BE munusŠAḪ SAG.DU U₃.TU-ma u-sur-tu₄ KUR DABat

(o 54) If a sow gives birth to an head and:4 a siege will afflict the land.

o 5555

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ la!-ḫi-im EN-ša U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ ⸢LUGUD₂⸣.DA-MEŠ

(o 55) If a sow gives birth and (the piglet) is covered in hair: its owner's days will become short.

o 5656

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ la!-bi-iš E₂ EN-ša₂ BIRaḫ

(o 56) If a sow gives birth and (the piglet) is coated with hair: its owner's household will be scattered.

o 5757

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ la-aḫ-mu U₄-MEŠ EN-ša₂ GID₂.DA-MEŠ

(o 57) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in hair: its owner's days will become long.

o 5858

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ BABBAR la-aḫ-mu U₄-MEŠ EN-ša₂ KI DINGIR ga-am-ru

(o 58) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in white hair: its owner's days are ended with the god.

o 5959

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ GE₆ la-aḫ-mu dan-na-tu₂ GAL₂-ma KUR ana KISLAḪ₃ NIGINur₂ EN munusŠAḪ KISLAḪ₃ IGImar

(o 59) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in black hair: there will be distress and the land will gather into the fortress; the sow's owner will experience distress.

o 6060

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ SA₅ la-aḫ-mu E₂ EN-ša₂ ul-tab-bar

(o 60) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in red hair: its owner's household will live long.

o 6161

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ GUN₃ la-aḫ-mu LUGAL ŠU₂ ina KUR GAL₂-ma KUR DU₁₀ab E₂ u EN-šu₂ ul-tab-bar

(o 61) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in multicoloured hair: there will be a king of the world in the land and the land will be in good condition; the household and its owner will live long.

o 6262

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ SIG₇ la-aḫ-mu U₄ ina ŠA₃.ḪUL DU-MEŠ

(o 62) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in yellow hair: the days will pass in sorrow.

o 6363

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ dan-niš la-aḫ-mu LUGAL ŠU₂ ina KUR GAL₂-ma ŠA₃ KUR! DU₁₀ab

(o 63) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are heavily covered in hair: there will be a king of the world in the land and the interior of the land will become in good condition.

o 6464

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂ BABBAR la-aḫ-mu KUR KISLAḪ₃ IGImar

(o 64) If a sow gives birth and (the piglets) are covered in white hair: the land will experience distress.

o 6565

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma UZU ina SAG.KI-šu₂ GIM rit-tu₂ a-rik LUGAL dan-nu ina KUR GAL₂-ma KUR un-na-aš

(o 65) If a sow gives birth and flesh on its (the piglet's) forehead is as long as a hand: there will be a mighty king in the land, but the land will weaken.

o 6666

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma UZU ina SAG.KI-šu₂ GIM ŠU.SI a-rik KI.MIN<(LUGAL)> dan-nu ina KUR GAL₂-ma KUR un-na-aš

(o 66) If a sow gives birth and flesh on the (piglet's) forehead is as long as a finger: ditto (there will be a mighty king in the land, but the land will weaken).

o 6767

BE munusŠAḪ AM U₃.TU ZIut EREN₂ni ma-at-ti ana KUR UMUŠ KUR MANni

(o 67) If a sow gives birth to a wild bull: attack of numerous troops against the land; decisions for the land will change.

o 6868

BE munusŠAḪ GU₄ U₃.TU LUGAL GABA.RI ul TUKUši

(o 68) If a sow gives birth to an ox: the king will have no opponent.

o 6969

BE munusŠAḪ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU KI.MIN<(LUGAL)> GABA.RI ul TUKUši AN.GE₆ d⸢20⸣

(o 69) If a sow gives birth to a lion: the king will have no opponent; solar eclipse.

o 7070

BE munusŠAḪ UR.BAR.RA U₃.TU UMUŠ KUR i-ša₂-an-ni E₂ EN-ša₂ i-na-qar

(o 70) If a sow gives birth to a wolf: decisions for the land will change; its owner's house will be demolished.

o 7171

BE munusŠAḪ UR.GI₇ U₃.TU pu-uḫ₂-pu-uḫ₂-ḫu ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 71) If a sow gives birth to a dog: there will be strife in the land.

o 7272

BE munusŠAḪ UR.KI U₃.TU EN munusŠAḪ ZAḪ₂

(o 72) If a sow gives birth to a badger: the sow's owner will disappear.

o 7373

BE munusŠAḪ KA₅.A U₃.TU TURer na-me-e

(o 73) If a sow gives birth to a fox: reduction of the flocks.

o 7474

BE munusŠAḪ PIRIG.TUR U₃.TU E₂ EN-šu₂ E₂.GAL UŠde

(o 74) If a sow gives birth to a leopard: the palace will appropriate its owner's house.

o 7575

BE munusŠAḪ dNIN.KILIM U₃.TU EN-šu₂ uḫ-<ta>-an-na-⸢aq⸣

(o 75) If a sow gives birth to a mongoose: its owner will be suffocated.

o 7676

BE munusŠAḪ NAM.LU₂.U₁₈.LU U₃.TU EN-šu₂ ZALAG₂ir DINGIR-šu₂ MU-šu₂ ana SIG₅ MUar₂ : ina na-me-e IRI-šu₂ uš-tal₂-⸢pat⸣

(o 76) If a sow gives birth to a human being: its owner will beam; his (personal) god will give (him) the best reputation; his city will be plundered of its pasturage.

o 7777

BE munusŠAḪ MUŠ U₃.TU KUR ana KISLAḪ₃ NIGINur₂

(o 77) If a sow gives birth to a snake: the land will gather into the fortress.

o 7878

BE munusŠAḪ ANŠE U₃.TU dEN.LIL₂ UMUŠ KUR.KUR ša RU AD RA BA NA ŠA TURar₂

(o 78) If a sow gives birth to a donkey: Ellil will diminish the decisions of the lands that ....

o 7979

BE munusŠAḪ ANŠE.KUR.RA U₃.TU KUR EN MAN-ma TUKUši

(o 79) If a sow gives birth to a horse: the land will acquire another lord.

o 8080

BE munusŠAḪ UGU.DUL.BI U₃.TU ZIut ELAM.MAki KUR ḫu-ša₂-aḫ-ḫu IGImar

(o 80) If a sow gives birth to an ape: attack of Elam; the land will experience famine.

o 8181

BE munusŠAḪ ŠAḪ.GIŠ.GI U₃.TU EN-šu₂ ZALAG₂ir

(o 81) If a sow gives birth to a wild boar: its owner will beam.

o 8282

BE munusŠAḪ NU GU₄ U₃.TU tu₁₅ŠEG₃ LA₂-MEŠ EN-šu₂ A₂.TUKU TUKUši

(o 82) If a sow gives birth to an ox's genitalia: rain will decrease; its owner will make profit.

o 8383

BE munusŠAḪ mu-qa₂-ra U₃.TU KUR dan-na-ti IGImar

(o 83) If a sow gives birth to a muqāru: the land will experience distress.

o 8484

BE munusŠAḪ MURGU U₃.TU EN munusŠAḪ UŠ₂ E₂-BI BIRaḫ

(o 84) If a sow gives birth to a shoulder: the sow's owner will die; his household will be scattered.

o 8585

BE munusŠAḪ tak₂-ki-ri U₃.TU kur₃-ru₃ LA₂al

(o 85) If a sow gives birth to takkīru-garments: the GUR-unit will be weighed.

o 8686

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma UŠ₂-MEŠ EN-ša₂ ḫa-an-ṭiš INIM TUKUši

(o 86) If a sow gives birth (to piglets) and they die: its owner will quickly receive news.

o 8787

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma ul-la-nu-um-ma UŠ₂-MEŠ E₂ EN-ša₂ KI.MIN<(ḫa-an-ṭiš)> INIM TUKUši

(o 87) If a sow gives birth (to piglets) and they die immediately: its owner's household will receive news.

o 8888

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇ EN-ša₂ a-wa-tu₂ TUKUši u₂-lu SAḪAR.ŠUB.BAa SA₅

(o 88) If a sow gives birth and eats its offspring: its owner will receive news or he will be covered with leprosy.

o 8989

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ 1en GU₇ IBILA EN-ša₂ [...]

(o 89) If a sow gives birth and eats one of its offspring: its owner's heir [...].

o 9090

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ i-kul-ma in-na-dir lu u₂-na-šak! NA BI NIG₂.ŠU-šu₂ TILmar-ma DUMU-⸢MEŠ⸣ [...]

(o 90) If a sow gives birth and eats its offspring and goes wild or bites: that man will destroy his property and the sons [...].

o 9191

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ ŠEŠ.ŠEŠ i-ku-lu DUMU-MEŠ E₂.AD [...]

(o 91) If a sow gives birth and its offspring eat each other: the sons will [...] the paternal house.

Reverse
r 1r 1

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma is-sa-ḫir-ma DUMU-šu₂ i-kul E₂ NA BI su-un-qu DABbat E₂ ⸢BI⸣ [...]

(r 1) If a sow gives birth and turns aside and eats its offspring: famine will afflict that man's house; that house [...].

r 22

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma is-sa-ḫir-ma DUMU-šu₂ i-kul EN-šu₂ GAZ-ši-ma UZU-šu₂ GU₇ SAG.⸢KI?⸣ [...]

(r 2) If a sow gives birth and turns aside and eats its offspring: its(masc) owner will kill it and eat its(masc) meat; the forehead [...].

r 33

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma is-sa-ḫi-ir-ma si-li-is-sa i-kul IRI BI su-un-qu DABbat LU₂ UŠ₂ ina SILA ⸢ŠUB⸣ [...]

(r 3) If a sow gives birth and turns aside and eats its afterbirth: a famine will afflict that city; the man will die; in the street ... [...].

r 44

BE munusŠAḪ U₃.TU-ma si-li-is-sa <<MA>> ⸢GU₇?⸣ mu-ut EN-ša₂ MA [...]

(r 4) If a sow gives birth and eats its afterbirth: death of its owner; ... [...].

r 55

BE munusŠAḪ ša₂ ŠA₃-ša₂ ŠUBa ⸢kur₃⸣-ru₃ [LA₂]al

(r 5) If a sow has a miscarriage: the GUR-unit will be weighed.

r 66

BE munusŠAḪ si-li-is-sa na-ši-ik E₂ EN-ša₂ BIRaḫ

(r 6) If a sow is gnawing its afterbirth: its owner's household will be scattered.

r 77

BE munusŠAḪ ir-ta-na-kab ṣi-bit-ti EN-ša₂ i-pa-ṭar₂

(r 7) If a sow keeps mounting (sexually): its owner's captivity will be cancelled.

r 88

BE munusŠAḪ u NIG 1-TA.AM₃ U₃.TU-MEŠ E₂ BI BIRaḫ

(r 8) If a sow and a bitch gives birth at the same time: that household will be scattered.

r 99

BE munusŠAḪ la₃ U₃.TU ina E₂ LU₂ it-tab-ši E₂ EN-ša₂ ḪUL dan-nu IGImar

(r 9) If there is always an unfertile sow in a man's house: severe evil will appear (in) its owner's house.

r 1010

BE <<munus>>ŠAḪ SIG₂ la-ḫi-im LUGAL ŠU₂ ina KUR GAL₂-ma KUR ENel DINGIR-MEŠ ze-nu-tu₄ ana!(MIN) KUR GUR-ME-ni

(r 10) If a pig is covered in hair: there will be a king of the world in the land and he will rule over the land; angry gods will turn against the land.

r 1111

BE <<munus>>ŠAḪ SIG₂ BABBAR la-ḫi-im <ina> KUR dan-na-tu₄ IGImar

(r 11) If a pig is covered in white hair: distress will appear in the land.

r 1212

BE ŠAḪ SIG₂ GE₆ la-ḫi-im KUR i-ḫar-ru-ub E₂ BI ar-bu-tu₂ DUak

(r 12) If a pig is covered in black hair: the land will become deserted; that house will be laid waste.

r 1313

BE ŠAḪ SIG₂ SA₅ la-ḫi-[im] ⸢LUGAL⸣ ŠU₂ ina KUR GAL₂-ma!(IS) IRI-DIDLI <ana> kar-me DU-ME

(r 13) If a pig is covered in red hair: there will be a king of the world in the land and the cities will turn into ruin mounds.

r 1414

BE ŠAḪ SIG₂ SIG₇ la-⸢ḫi⸣-[im] UŠ₂ KUR NU GUR U₂ ana EN-šu₂ TE-am

(r 14) If a pig is covered in yellow hair: the dying one of the land will not recover; ... will approach its owner.

r 1515

BE ŠAḪ ina x E₂ EN [...]-ma ŠUBut ana IRI BI tam-ṭa₂-a-tu₄!(AT) GAR-MEŠ E₂ EN-ša₂ su-un-qu DABat

(r 15) If a pig [...] in ... the owner's house and falls: losses will take place in that city; famine will afflict its(fem) owner's house.

r 1616

BE ⸢ŠAḪ in?⸣-na-dir-⸢ma⸣ [...] [u₂]-na-šak E₂ EN-šu MANni

(r 16) If a pig goes wild and bites [...]: its owner's house will change.

r 1717

BE ŠAḪ ⸢ZU₂⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ i-kaṣ₃-ṣa-aṣ E₂ EN-šu₂ BIRaḫ

(r 17) If a pig grinds its teeth: its owner's household will be scattered.

r 1818

BE ŠAḪ ina TUR₃-šu₂ i-dam-mu-um UMUŠ E₂ EN-šu₂ KUR₂ir : MANni

(r 18) If a pig moans in its stall: decisions for its owner's house will become hostile (or): they will change.

r 1919

BE ŠAḪ ina ⸢E₂? EN?⸣-šu₂ id-da!(TA-)nam-ma-am EN-šu₂ UŠ₂-ma E₂-BI BIRaḫ

(r 19) If a pig keeps moaning in its owner's house: its owner will die and his household will be scattered.

r 2020

BE ŠAḪ <ina> IGI NA KA-šu₂ ip-te-ne-te DAM-su it-ta-na-a-a-ku

(r 20) If a pig keeps openning its mouth in front of a man: they will keep having sexual intercourse with his wife.

r 2121

BE ŠAḪ ana IGI EN-šu₂ i-ʾi₃-ir ana EN-šu₂ U.BARru₃ TE-am

(r 21) If a pig awakes in front of a man: a foreigner will approach its owner.

r 2222

BE ŠAḪ ina IGI EN-šu₂ ig?-lu-ut ina ⸢E₂?⸣ EN-šu₂ GABA.RAḪ.ḪA GAR!an

(r 22) If a pig trembles in front of its owner: panic will take place in its owner's house.

r 2323

BE ŠAḪ ina TUR₃ E₂ EN-šu₂ KI i-si₂₀-ir U.BARru₃ ša₂ IRI-MEŠ DUkam

(r 23) If a pig in the house's stall encloses its owner (in this) place: a city foreigner will come.

r 2424

BE ŠAḪ GIŠ ḪI LA ig-gu-uš-ma LU₂ im-ši-id KI DUku AŠ₂ IRI NU KURad₂

(r 24) If a pig go towards ... and crushes a man: wherever he goes, the city's wish will not be achieved.

r 2525

BE ŠAḪ iš-tu nar-ba-ṣi-šu₂ iš-še-gu EN-šu₂ i-na-kir me-neš-ti IGImar

(r 25) If a pig goes wild out of its lair: its owner will change; he will experience weakness.

r 2626

BE ŠAḪ ina E₂ EN-šu₂ KI GIŠ GIŠ MAR x-MEŠ ⸢LI?⸣ MA DAGAL TA DI MU KI ⸢E?⸣ LU₂ ni-ṣir-ti KUR ina SILA GARan

(r 26) If a pig ... in its owner's house ... and ... wide ... a man will put the treasure of the land in the street.

r 2727

BE ŠAḪ ana qe₂-reb ur-ši LU₂ KU₄ub a-se-er-ti ana E₂ EN-šu₂ KU₄ba

(r 27) If a pig enters into the middle of a man's bedroom: a confined woman will enter its owner's house.

r 2828

BE ŠAḪ nam-mi-ru-tu₂ ne₂-ḫi-iš i-te-ne₂-ep-pu-uš GIG ina a-ḫi-ti UŠ₂

(r 28) If a fattened pig keeps behaving calmly: a sick person will die in misfortune.

r 2929

BE ŠAḪ GI na-ši ZIut ⸢ZAḪ₂!⸣ ḪI.GAR u EN ḪI.GAR GAL₂ši : ZItu₂ GAL₂ši

(r 29) If a pig is carrying reed: there will be an attack of fugitives; there will be a rebellion and a leader of the rebellion (or): there will be an attack.

r 3030

BE ŠAḪ GI na-ši-ma x x x [x] ŠU₂ i-šar₂-ru₃

(r 30) If a pig is carrying reed and ...: he will become rich.

r 3131

BE ŠAḪ GI na-ši-ma iš-tu E₂ EN-šu₂ E₃a EN-šu₂ MEŠti I.BI₂.ZA IGImar

(r 31) If a pig is carrying reed and goes out from its owner's house: its owner will experience plenty of loss.

r 3232

BE ŠAḪ giDUR la₃ x ana E₂ EN-šu₂ KU₄ub ⸢EN⸣-šu₂ ne!-me-la₅ IGImar

(r 32) If a pig does not ... a rope (and) enters into its owner's house: its owner will see profit.

r 3333

BE ŠAḪ giDUR na-<ši>-ma iš-tu E₂ EN-šu₂ E₃a ⸢EN⸣-šu₂ I.BI₂.ZA IGImar

(r 33) If a pig is carrying a rope and goes out from its owner's house: its owner will experience loss.

r 3434

BE ŠAḪ PA gišGIŠIMMAR na-ši me-ḫu-u₂ ZIam

(r 34) If a pig is carrying a date palm branch: a storm wind will arise.

r 3535

BE ŠAḪ ŠU.SAR na-ši SU.GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 35) If a pig is carrying a string (of palm fibre): there will be a famine in the land.

r 3636

BE ŠAḪ giGILIM na-ši-ma ina SILA DU.DU GANBA GAL₂ši

(r 36) If a pig is carrying a reed bundle and roams around in the street: there will be trade.

r 3737

BE ŠAḪ giGILIM na-ši-ma im-me-x ZI me-ḫe-e

(r 37) If a pig is carrying a reed bundle and ...: onset of a storm wind.

r 3838

BE ŠAḪ giGILIM na-ši-ma iš-tu KA₂ E₂ EN-šu₂ E₃ E₂ EN-šu₂ NIG₂.TUK

(r 38) If a pig is carrying a reed bundle and goes out from the gate of its owner's house: its owner's house will become rich.

r 3939

BE ŠAḪ giGILIM na-ši-ma iš-tu E₂ EN-šu₂ ana KA₂ E₃ EN-šu₂ I.BI₂.ZA IGImar

(r 39) If a pig is carrying a reed bundle and goes out from its owner's house by the gate: its owner will experience loss.

r 4040

BE ŠAḪ SIG₂ ina KA-šu₂ na-ši MI ⸢ḪI?⸣ ana? ⸢UŠ?⸣ BAL qa₂-ti

(r 40) If a pig is carrying hair in its mouth ...: the reign is ended.

r 4141

BE ŠAḪ ana IGI LU₂ SIG₂-⸢su ŠUB⸣-ma ki-i-ṣi-⸢ir?⸣ E₂ NA BI ar-bu-tu₂ DUak

(r 41) If a pig sheds its hair in front of a man and it (forms) a knot: the house of that man will be laid waste.

r 4242

BE ŠAḪ [...] ⸢LU? ŠU₂⸣ GIŠ? ḪU? U₄? GIŠ TI ŠU₂ IL BU AḪ ina IRI U.BAR E₃

(r 42) If a pig [...] ...: ... a foreigner will go out of the city.

r 4343

BE ŠAḪ [...] x-šu₂ ⸢MAR?⸣ DAM LU₂ in-na-ak

(r 43) If a pig ... [...]: the man's wife will have sexual intercourse.

r 4444

BE ŠAḪ ⸢IGI?⸣ x x ⸢SU⸣.GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 44) If a pig [...] (in) front ...: there will be hunger in the country.

r 4545

BE ŠAḪ [...] x x ša₂ la uš-te-er-su-u₂ GU₇

(r 45) If a pig ... [...]: he will eat what he has not prepared.

r 4646

BE ŠAḪ ina? x-šu₂ x x EN-šu₂ mim-mu-šu₂ ana IGI-šu₂ DUak

(r 46) If a pig ... in ...: all its owner's (possessions) will grow larger.

r 4747

BE ŠAḪ ina x ⸢NA?⸣ x ⸢MAN? GU⸣ EN-šu₂ MANni

(r 47) If a pig ... in ...: its owner will change.

r 4848

BE ŠAḪ ina x x x x ŠUB [...] x ZI me-ḫe-e

(r 48) If a pig ... in ...: onset of a storm wind.

r 4949

BE ŠAḪ ina [...] ŠU₂ KI NA ⸢SAḪAR?⸣ i-se-er U.BARru₃ KU₄am

(r 49) If a pig [...] in [...] (and) smears (itself with) earth by a man: a foreigner will enter.

r 5050

BE ŠAḪ ina x [...] ana IGI x U.BARru₃ iš-tu E₂ E₃

(r 50) If a pig [...] in ... in front ...: a foreigner will leave the house.

r 5151

BE ŠAḪ ina E₂ ⸢bu⸣-uš ⸢EN⸣-šu₂ KI.MIN UKU₂ E₂ BI BIRaḫ

(r 51) If a pig is stinking in the house: its owner ditto; he will become poor; that household will be scattered.

r 5252

BE ŠAḪ x x IŠ [x?] DA MA [x] x E₂ BI BIRaḫ

(r 52) If a pig [...] ... [...] ... that household will be scattered.

r 5353

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ id-da-ri-ir-ma ana ⸢E₂ LU₂⸣ KU₄ er-re-bi ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄ E₂ BI ŠUBdi

(r 53) If a pig not belonging to him roams free and enters a man's house: intruders will enter the man's house; that house will be abandoned.

r 5454

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma u₂-qal-⸢lil!⸣ ar-nu TUKUši DABbat

(r 54) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and defiles (it): he will suffer a punishment; he will be held captive.

r 5555

BE ŠAḪ ana E₂ <<ina>> KU₄-ma mim-ma il-qe₂ E₂ BI lu-up-nu DABbat

(r 55) If a pig enters into a house and takes something: poverty will befall (on) that house.

r 5656

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma mim-ma ana KA₂ E₃ ti-bu-um

(r 56) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and takes something out of the gate: attack.

r 5757

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma LU₂ iš-li U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ

(r 57) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and tears a man('s skin): his days will become short.

r 5858

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma ana [...] x si-ḫi-il!(TUM₃-)tu₂ UZU ana E₂ BI GARan

(r 58) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and ... to [...]: pain of the flesh will afflict that house.

r 5959

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma E₂ [u₂]-na-pil TIL u₄-me

(r 59) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and forages in the house: end of days.

r 6060

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma A-MEŠ ⸢it-bu⸣-uk ŠA₃-BI la₃ DU₁₀.GA GAR-šu

(r 60) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and spills water: (that man) will not be provided with satisfaction.

r 6161

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma ⸢še-am?⸣ [x] ŠA₃-BI la₃ DU₁₀.GA GAR-šu

(r 61) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and [...] barley: (that man) will not be provided with satisfaction.

r 6262

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma x [...] x UM NA BI mu-lem-min₃ a-[ma-ti] ⸢TUKU⸣ši

(r 62) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and [...]: that man will acquire a slanderer.

r 6363

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄-ma LU₂ x x TI MU 1-KAM x x x

(r 63) If a pig not belonging to him enters a man's house and the man [...]: ... one year ....

r 6464

BE ŠAḪ la šu-u₂ ana E₂ LU₂ u₄-mi-šam KU₄-MEŠ lu-up-nu DABbat

(r 64) If a pig not belonging to him keeps entering the man's house every day: poverty will befall (him).

r 6565

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ it-te-ki-pu-ma ana IGI LU₂ in-neš-ru NA BI ni-ziq-ti i-ṭa-ḫi-šu₂

(r 65) If pigs butt each other and advance against a man: that man will be approached by grief.

r 6666

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ [i]-mi-du-ma ina SILA ⸢up⸣-taḫ-⸢ḫa-ru⸣-ma ⸢ana⸣ LU₂ it-ta-na-ak-ṣa-ṣu₂ IRI BI BIR[aḫ]

(r 66) If pigs become numerous and are gathered in the street and grind (their teeth) against a man: that city will be scattered.

r 6767

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ ⸢ZU₂?⸣-[MEŠ]-⸢šu₂?-nu⸣ la₃ i-ka-ṣa-ṣu₂ ŠUBti₃ EREN₂

(r 67) If the pigs do not grind their teeth: defeat of the troops.

r 6868

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ ina SILA [ir]-⸢ta-na-qu⸣-du ZI.GA TU₁₅ šum-ma ZIut gišGIGIR GI? x

(r 68) If the pigs keep gamboling in the street: onset of the wind; if an attack of the chariotry ... [...].

r 6969

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ ina SILA KAŠ₄-MEŠ [x] ⸢KUN⸣-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ⸢ta⸣-[ru]-u₂ ZIut EREN₂ni EREN₂ni SAG A.ŠA₃-šu₂ ⸢KUR⸣ [x]

(r 69) If pigs run in the street and their tails are turned (down): attack of the troops; the troops will reach the edge of his field.

r 7070

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ ina SILA KAŠ₄-MEŠ-⸢ma KUN⸣-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ana ⸢ḫal⸣-[li]-šu₂-nu GUR-ME ZIut EREN₂ni [...]

(r 70) If pigs run in the street and their tails turn to their upper thigh: attack of the troops.

r 7171

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ i-qar-ru-ru [...] x UKU₂

(r 71) If pigs roll [...] ... he will become poor.

r 7272

BE ŠAḪ-MEŠ SIG₄ E₂ i!(U₂-)qar-ru-ru!(MAḪ) [...] UKU₂

(r 72) If pigs roll in the mudbricks for houses [...] he will become poor.

r 7373

BE ŠAḪ.GIŠ.GI I DA x x x x x [...]

(r 73) If a wild boar ... [...].

r 7474

BE ŠAḪ.GIŠ.GI a-na IRI KU₄ub [...]

(r 74) If a wild boar enters into the city [...].

r 7575

BE ŠAḪ.GIŠ.GI a-da-at-tu₂ na-ši-ma [...]

(r 75) If a wild boar is carrying an adattu-reed and [...].

r 7676

BE ŠAḪ.GIŠ.GI ina IGI KA₂.GAL ⸢GAZ?⸣ [...]

(r 76) If a wild boar is killed in front of the city gate [...].

r 7777

BE ŠAḪ.GIŠ.GI KUR im-ta-lu-u₂ [...] ŠU₂ [...] LUGAL [...]

(r 77) If wild boars keep filling the land [...] ... the king [...].

(catchline)
r 7878

BE NIG 1 U₃.TU E₂ BI BIRaḫ [x (x)] ZAḪ₂ LUGAL BI ana KUR BI x GAR NU GAR

(r 78) If a bitch gives birth to 1 (puppy): that household will be scattered; [...] it will be destroyed; that king will not settle ... for that land.

(colophon)
r 7979

ŠU.NIGIN 2 UŠ 48-AM₃ MU ⸢ŠID⸣-[BI DUB] 22-KAM BE iz-bu la₃ AL.TIL

(r 79) Total: 2 sixty 48 lines, [counted. Tablet] 22 (of the Series): "If an anomaly"; not completed.

r 8080

⸢GIM⸣ SUMUN-šu SAR-ma IGI.TAB x x [...] x ŠU!(BA) d<60>—u₂-ṣu-ur₂!? [A] lu₂⸢E₂?.KUR!(EŠ—)za!(GAR-)[kir]

(r 80) Written and checked according to its original ... [...] hand of Anu-uṣur, descendant of Ekur-zakir.

1 The subject of this stative is missing.

2 The verb is missing here.

3 "its " is masculine here . It is the same on lines 52-53 ; 74-76 ; 81-82 and reverse 2.

4 A verb or stative is probably missing here.

Created by Marie-Françoise Besnier for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2009 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P348698
